feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: >fighting Gerson on my Steam Deck while the toilet >I won >put the seat down and…
entry.content: >fighting Gerson on my Steam Deck while the toilet >I won >put the seat down and put the deck on top of it to wash my hands >starts slipping off >manage to snag it before it hits the floor >scratched the back part of the housing from shoving it into the toilet on reflex to save it Now I have a battle scar from Gerson on my Steam Deck wicked

entry.title: /o/ Auto: In what YEAR do you estimate Mercedes will stop producing shitty interiors? >why…
entry.content: In what YEAR do you estimate Mercedes will stop producing shitty interiors? >why did this happen well, adjusted for inflation Mercedes are actually cheaper than before by a significant margin, especially the volume upper models like e and s class, but that's only in Europe and North America. I think in the Middle East, Asia and Africa there are zany tariffs and other fees that can tack on 50k+ to the price of these cars so the real price is much higher and presumably such customers are more conc

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Static site + GitHub repo critique
entry.content: Built a static project site for an open-source Blender model/source release. Looking for technical critique: does the site explain the project fast enough, does the GitHub repo look credible, is the source-release CTA obvious, and does anything about the presentation look scammy/vaporware? Site: https://highfrontierlogistics.com/ Repo: https://github.com/highfrontierlogi stics/orbital-tender Not asking for praise — what would make you immediately close the tab?

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Do you feel that Ween reached their full musical potential in their careers? The…
entry.content: Do you feel that Ween reached their full musical potential in their careers? They don't have a bad album in my opinion: The Mollusk and Quebec are classics. Yet if they weren't plagued by personal demons, could they have reached a greater zenith? Perhaps it just wouldn't be Ween anymore if they weren't, in some parts, slacker degenerates.

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: I just admire your beauty and your people. I don't have any romantic interest in…
entry.content: I just admire your beauty and your people. I don't have any romantic interest in you, I just wish I were you It's fascinated how you defeated the Roman Empire and built civilizations greater than it. Today, you're richer than Mediterranean countries. You're also more beautiful than them. I'm just a Luso-Shitzilian against my will, I'll never be like you. If I could choose my body, I'd choose to be you, but I can't because this world is trash. You're lucky, I'm the one who should kill myself, not

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: I wish I wasn't born in Canada it's an awful degenerative wasteland I'm not real…
entry.content: I wish I wasn't born in Canada it's an awful degenerative wasteland I'm not really sure about what race I wish I was, because mostly spending lots of time thinking about stuff like that is a complete waste of time and completely impractical. I guess I just wish I had a giant dick, a genius I.Q. and physically adept and good at things like sports. That would be cool. But then that's why I wish I wasn't born on the continent at all. I wish I was born in a magical kingdom, like Hyrule or something

entry.title: /mu/ Music: A rather ordinary-sounding white Southern boogie band, except that this one has …
entry.content: A rather ordinary-sounding white Southern boogie band, except that this one has its roots in pop--they began as the Candymen, the greatest cover band in history. Only they actually began as Roy Orbison's backup band. As you might imagine, they're tighter and slicker than your ordinary boogie band, which all things considered is a small boon. I hope somebody on Capricorn covers "Wrong." And I hope they find something as good as, oh, "Good Vibrations" to cover themselves. C+

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: I'm a Communist, I've read Marx, and I'm a full supporter of class struggle, I'm…
entry.content: I'm a Communist, I've read Marx, and I'm a full supporter of class struggle, I'm even a member of the CPUSA. But, I suppose because of the domination fetish I have, I always find myself fantasizing about being an extreme leftist activist participating in labor organizations, workers' strikes, etc, fighting against capital, then going to the hotel room reserved by the bourgeois old man who's going to dominate me. I want to be dominated by a bourgeois, old, fat, powerful man, literally sodomized.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: So I am thinking of getting a controller: I am thinking between GameSir Cyclone …
entry.content: So I am thinking of getting a controller: I am thinking between GameSir Cyclone 2 , GAMESIR G7 PRO and Flydigi Vader 5 Pro as they have almost the same price in my country and they have almost the same functionality too, which is a bit strange. My target is PC only, but i do not know if i would use turbo or macros so its all a bit hard to decide. I guess I want a general controller tier list and discussion thread.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: STOP! TELL ME WHAT YOU PLAYED THIS WEEK!
entry.content: Or you're a faggot. I've been playing Command & Conquer: RA1, Quake 3, Ninja Gaiden 2, GTA Vice City, CS: Source with bouts of Gears of War 3, and Dead Rising 2. Personally, I think DR2 is superior to DR1, and GoW3 is superior to the series overall. This goes for Quake 3, and maybe Vice City - as I think San Andreas is quite fun, but suffers from bloat. But what about you?

entry.title: /int/ International: Jane Eyre,Wuthering Heights As far as I felt when I was in elementary school, th…
entry.content: Jane Eyre,Wuthering Heights As far as I felt when I was in elementary school, the main characters of Wuthering Heights looked selfish and mentally weird. That's why I like Jane Eyre more personally, But why are British novels always the stories of aristocrats? Is there a law where the writer is punished if the worker or the maid is the main character?

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Movie tropes based on reality
entry.content: >took my glasses off because it rained a lot while I was commuting and forgot to bring my wipes waiting for them to dry >suddenly co-workers start chatting with me a lot more >suddenly get 4x in a row clients who nearly immediately buy the crappy contracts im selling them >put my glasses back on >co-workers quit yapping and suddenly clients actually start pushing back on my offers Damn wtf, the movies didn't lie. I really need to switch to contacts.
